example_cases.description: Parse new JSONL message appended to session file and emit on_jsonl_append event with correct JsonlMessage fields
example_cases.input: Append one valid JSONL line {"role":"human","content":"fix the login bug"} to ~/.claude/projects/abc123/sessions/sess.jsonl
example_cases.expected_output: on_jsonl_append emitted within 2s with new_lines containing one JsonlMessage: role=human, content='fix the login bug', session_id parsed from file path, project_hash='abc123'
example_cases.setup: Create mock ~/.claude/projects/abc123/sessions/ directory with empty sess.jsonl file. Start watcher with start_watching.

example_cases.description: Watcher maintains file watchers on all active Claude Code session directories while running
example_cases.input: Start watcher with claude_base_dir pointing to a directory containing 2 project subdirectories, each with a sessions/ folder
example_cases.expected_output: Both session directories are being watched. Appending a line to either file triggers on_jsonl_append.
example_cases.setup: Create two project dirs under mock base dir, each with sessions/*.jsonl files.

example_cases.description: Detect JSONL file rotation (old file deleted/renamed, new file created) and resume watching without data loss
example_cases.input: Rename active sess.jsonl to sess.jsonl.bak, create new sess.jsonl, append a line to the new file
example_cases.expected_output: on_file_rotation emitted with old_path and new_path. Subsequent append to new file triggers on_jsonl_append.
example_cases.setup: Start watcher with an active JSONL file being monitored.

example_cases.description: Idle watcher with no file changes consumes <1% CPU
example_cases.input: Start watcher on 3 project directories, then measure CPU for 10 seconds with no file changes
example_cases.expected_output: Average CPU usage <1% over the measurement period
example_cases.setup: Start watcher. Use process stats (getrusage or /proc/self/stat) to measure CPU time before and after 10s sleep.

example_cases.description: Detect git commit event in monitored project directory and emit on_git_signal
example_cases.input: In a monitored project directory, run 'git commit' (create a file, git add, git commit -m 'test')
example_cases.expected_output: on_git_signal emitted within 5s with signal_type='commit', project_dir matching the repo, metadata.branch and metadata.message populated
example_cases.setup: Create a temp git repo in a monitored project directory. Configure watcher to poll this directory.

example_cases.description: Detect git push event and emit on_git_signal with signal_type=push
example_cases.input: In a monitored project directory with a remote, run 'git push'
example_cases.expected_output: on_git_signal emitted with signal_type='push', metadata.remote_url populated
example_cases.setup: Create temp git repo with a local bare remote. Push a commit.

example_cases.description: 30 minutes of no git activity emits idle signal for previously active context
example_cases.input: After a git commit is detected, simulate 30 minutes of inactivity (mock clock or accelerated timer)
example_cases.expected_output: on_git_signal emitted with signal_type='idle' for that project_dir
example_cases.setup: Configure watcher with an injectable clock. Trigger a commit event, then advance clock by 30 minutes.

example_cases.description: Git polling rate limited to max once per 30 seconds per context
example_cases.input: Trigger 5 rapid git-related fs events within 5 seconds for the same project dir
example_cases.expected_output: poll_git_status is called at most once within the 30s window. Subsequent events are debounced.
example_cases.setup: Start watcher with a git repo. Instrument poll_git_status call count.

example_cases.description: Batch of multiple JSONL lines appended at once emits single on_jsonl_append with all lines
example_cases.input: Append 5 valid JSONL lines in a single write to session file
example_cases.expected_output: on_jsonl_append emitted with new_lines containing exactly 5 JsonlMessage items, in order
example_cases.setup: Start watcher with active session file. Append 5 lines atomically.

example_cases.description: Invalid JSONL line (malformed JSON) is skipped without crashing the watcher
example_cases.input: Append one invalid line '{not valid json' followed by one valid JSONL line
example_cases.expected_output: on_jsonl_append emitted with only the valid message. Invalid line is logged as warning.
example_cases.setup: Start watcher with active session file.

example_cases.description: poll_git_status returns error when git CLI is not found on PATH
example_cases.input: Call poll_git_status with project_dir pointing to a git repo, but with git removed from PATH
example_cases.setup: Create temp git repo. Override PATH to exclude git binary.

example_cases.description: poll_git_status returns error when directory is not a git repo
example_cases.input: Call poll_git_status with project_dir pointing to a plain directory (no .git)
example_cases.setup: Create a temp directory without git init.

example_cases.description: New project directory appearing while watcher is running gets auto-detected
example_cases.input: While watcher is running, create a new project directory with sessions/*.jsonl under the base dir, then append a line
example_cases.expected_output: The new directory is detected and on_jsonl_append is emitted for the appended line
example_cases.setup: Start watcher with base dir containing one project. Then add a second project dir dynamically.
